title: Support template unit files in podman generate systemd

comments.body:
> Thanks! Please add tests to pkg/systemd/generate (unit test) and to test/system/250-systemd.bats.

Unit tests are still missing. Have a look at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/systemd/generate/containers_test.go where the _entire_ output is checked.

The added system tests are a good start but we need to also exercise such a template and run it. Generating them is not sufficient to catch regressions.
@Luap99 added the remote part to the code. I am going now to deal with @vrothberg comment about adding unit tests.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *boaz0*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@boaz0 what is going on with this one?
Close to finish with this. I will resolve conflicts and merge my changes. Sorry for the delay.

@vrothberg & @rhatdan - I addressed all the comments except to the one about testing.
I am now figuring out how to run systemctl in `bats`.
Thanks

![](https://media.giphy.com/media/j3o8Ikxg8bIMNg23Cg/giphy.gif)

>figuring out how to run systemctl in bats.

@boaz0 if the existing code in `250-systemd.bats` isn't sufficient, there are plenty of other `systemctl` examples in other `.bats` tests. And if none of those help, please feel free to reach out to me.

@edsantiago I get the following error when trying to run systemctl:
```sh
$ systemctl start /run/user/1000/systemd/user/podman_test_1tTri4fRvS@1.service
Failed to start run-user-1000-systemd-user-podman_test_1tTri4fRvS\x401.service.mount: Unit run-user-1000-systemd-user-podman_test_1tTri4fRvS\x401.service.mount not found.
```

However, if I move it to my `~/.config/systemd/user/podman_test_1tTri4fRvS@.service and run it:
```sh
$ systemctl --user start podman_test_1tTri4fRvS@1.service
```
It works.

@boaz0 there are too many unknowns here. In your excerpts, are you showing actual commands you ran in your shell, or output from the BATS tests? It's important because `systemctl` in the BATS tests is a shortcut for "if root, then systemctl, if user, then systemctl --user". And your first command is missing the required "--user". Your first command also includes unnecessary absolute path components. Also, it's not clear if you ran `systemctl daemon-reload`.

Can you try:
* Create the unit file as `/run/user/1000/systemd/user/podman_test_1tTri4fRvS@1.service`
* run `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
* run `systemctl start podman_test_1tTri4fRvS@1.service`
@edsantiago thanks for the help. I believe that fixes it. :crossed_fingers:

@vrothberg can you review this again.
Thanks.
